steps.title: Identify the comparison
steps.content: Fodor's hypothesis treats low-level systems as modular but not high-level systems. Carruthers's MMH expands modularity to all cognitive systems.

steps.title: Compare the choices
steps.content: Choice B gives sequence without a real comparison; Choice C falsely says they differ on low-level systems; Choice D misstates Fodor's view. Choice A accurately contrasts Fodor's narrower scope with MMH's broader scope. The answer is A.
